536	def aggregate_per_template(results: dict[str, QuestionResult]) -> dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, float]]]:
537	    """Aggregate dimension scores per (template, system) — needed for match-or-beat verdict."""
538	    agg: dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, list[float]]]] = {}
539	    for r in results.values():
540	        agg.setdefault(r.template, {})
541	        for sys_name, scores in r.scores.items():
542	            agg[r.template].setdefault(sys_name, {})
543	            for s in scores:
544	                if s.score == s.score:
545	                    agg[r.template][sys_name].setdefault(s.dimension, []).append(s.score)
546	    return {
547	        tmpl: {
548	            sys_name: {dim: (sum(vals) / len(vals)) for dim, vals in dims.items() if vals}
549	            for sys_name, dims in tmpl_data.items()
550	        }
551	        for tmpl, tmpl_data in agg.items()
552	    }
553	
554	
555	# Match-or-beat is computed against this set; D3/D4/D6/D7 are POLARIS-unique
556	# (incumbents will be N/A). D1+D2+D5+D8 are the cross-comparable dimensions.
557	MATCH_OR_BEAT_DIMENSIONS = {
558	    "factual_accuracy", "citation_health", "refusal_calibration", "sycophancy_resistance",
559	}
560	
561	
562	CARNEY_TEMPLATES = frozenset({
563	    "clinical", "policy", "tech", "due_diligence",
564	    "ai_sovereignty", "canada_us", "workforce", "custom",
565	})
566	
567	
568	def compute_match_or_beat(
569	    per_template_per_system: dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, float]]],
570	    primary_system: str = "polaris_v6",
571	    competitors: tuple[str, ...] = ("chatgpt_5_5_pro_dr", "gemini_3_1_pro_dr"),
572	    require_live: bool = True,
573	) -> dict[str, Any]:
574	    """Per-template match-or-beat verdict with INSUFFICIENT-DATA guards.
575	
576	    Per Plan v13 §F (no SILENT fallback): refuse to emit APPROVE if
577	    competitor data is missing or all-error. A POLARIS "win" against
578	    zeros from missing competitor responses is meaningless.
579	
580	    Per-template states:
581	      - "polaris_win": polaris_avg > best_comp_avg, both non-zero on ALL comparable dims
582	      - "tie": polaris_avg == best_comp_avg, both non-zero
583	      - "polaris_loss": polaris_avg < best_comp_avg
584	      - "insufficient_data": competitor data missing OR all-error OR polaris missing dims
585	
586	    Verdict rules:
587	      - APPROVE: ≥ 6/8 templates polaris_win, AND zero templates in insufficient_data
588	      - BELOW_BAR: < 6/8 wins but data complete
589	      - INSUFFICIENT_DATA: any template in insufficient_data
590	    """
591	    out_per_template: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
592	    win_count = 0  # Carney templates only — extras don't contribute to verdict
593	    extra_win_count = 0  # extras tracked separately for transparency
594	    loss_count = 0
595	    tie_count = 0
596	    insufficient_count = 0
597	    required_dims = MATCH_OR_BEAT_DIMENSIONS
598	
599	    for tmpl, tmpl_data in per_template_per_system.items():
600	        is_carney_template = tmpl in CARNEY_TEMPLATES
601	        polaris_dims = tmpl_data.get(primary_system, {})
602	        polaris_present_dims = [d for d in required_dims if d in polaris_dims]
603	        polaris_complete = len(polaris_present_dims) == len(required_dims)
604	        polaris_avg = (sum(polaris_dims[d] for d in polaris_present_dims) / len(polaris_present_dims)) if polaris_present_dims else 0.0
605	
606	        # Competitor must have ALL required comparable dimensions present
607	        # (not just one) AND non-zero data (not all-error). Partial-dimension
608	        # competitors give POLARIS unfair partial-credit comparison.
609	        best_comp_avg = 0.0
610	        best_comp = None
611	        comp_with_data = []
612	        for comp in competitors:
613	            comp_dims = tmpl_data.get(comp, {})
614	            comparable_c = [comp_dims[d] for d in required_dims if d in comp_dims]
615	            # Require ALL required dims present AND at least one >0 (not all-error)
616	            if len(comparable_c) == len(required_dims) and any(v > 0.0 for v in comparable_c):
617	                comp_with_data.append(comp)
618	                comp_avg = sum(comparable_c) / len(comparable_c)
619	                if comp_avg > best_comp_avg:
620	                    best_comp_avg = comp_avg
621	                    best_comp = comp
622	
623	        # Insufficient-data guard (Plan v13 §F):
624	        # require BOTH competitors (chatgpt + gemini) AND polaris complete.
625	        # The match-or-beat bar is a 3-way side-by-side; allowing a template
626	        # to count when only 1 of 2 competitors has data lets POLARIS "win"
627	        # against a single competitor when the spec requires BOTH.
628	        missing_competitors = [c for c in competitors if c not in comp_with_data]
629	        if not polaris_complete:
630	            state = "insufficient_data"
631	            reason = f"polaris missing dims: {sorted(set(required_dims) - set(polaris_present_dims))}"
632	        elif missing_competitors:
633	            state = "insufficient_data"
634	            reason = f"competitor(s) missing comparable data: {missing_competitors} (3-way side-by-side requires all)"
635	        elif polaris_avg == 0.0 and best_comp_avg == 0.0:
636	            # Both at zero → likely dry-run or all-error
637	            state = "insufficient_data"
638	            reason = "polaris and competitor both 0.0 (likely dry-run or all-error; refusing to declare winner)"
639	        else:
640	            # Match-or-beat semantics: ties count as wins (POLARIS matches the
641	            # competitor → satisfies the "match" half of "match-or-beat").
642	            # ONLY Carney templates contribute to win_count (verdict bar);
643	            # extras are tracked in extra_win_count for transparency.
644	            if polaris_avg > best_comp_avg:
645	                state = "polaris_win"
646	                if is_carney_template: win_count += 1
647	                else: extra_win_count += 1
648	            elif polaris_avg == best_comp_avg:
649	                state = "polaris_match"
650	                tie_count += 1
651	                if is_carney_template: win_count += 1
652	                else: extra_win_count += 1
653	            else:
654	                state = "polaris_loss"; loss_count += 1
655	            reason = ""
656	
657	        if state == "insufficient_data":
658	            insufficient_count += 1
659	
660	        out_per_template[tmpl] = {
661	            "is_carney_template": is_carney_template,
662	            "polaris_avg": polaris_avg,
663	            "best_competitor": best_comp,
664	            "best_competitor_avg": best_comp_avg,
665	            "competitors_with_data": comp_with_data,
666	            "polaris_dimensions_present": polaris_present_dims,
667	            "state": state,
668	            # Match-or-beat: BOTH polaris_win and polaris_match satisfy the bar
669	            "polaris_wins": state in ("polaris_win", "polaris_match"),
670	            "counts_toward_verdict": is_carney_template and state in ("polaris_win", "polaris_match"),
671	            "delta": polaris_avg - best_comp_avg,
672	            "insufficient_data_reason": reason,
673	        }
674	
675	    template_count = len(per_template_per_system)
676	    present_templates = set(per_template_per_system.keys())
677	    missing_carney = CARNEY_TEMPLATES - present_templates
678	    extra_templates = present_templates - CARNEY_TEMPLATES
679	
680	    # APPROVE bar: per Plan v13 §F + rubric §4, requires
681	    # (a) EXACTLY the 8 Carney templates present in the run (no missing, no extras); AND
682	    # (b) require_live mode active (dry-run has no real data to verdict on); AND
683	    # (c) ≥6 of 8 templates polaris_win OR polaris_match; AND
684	    # (d) zero templates in insufficient_data state.
685	    REQUIRED_WIN_COUNT = 6
686	    if not LIVE_MODE and require_live:
687	        verdict = "DRY_RUN_NO_VERDICT"
688	    elif missing_carney:
689	        verdict = "INCOMPLETE_TEMPLATES"
690	    elif insufficient_count > 0:
691	        verdict = "INSUFFICIENT_DATA"
692	    elif win_count >= REQUIRED_WIN_COUNT:
693	        verdict = "APPROVE"
694	    else:
695	        verdict = "BELOW_BAR"
696	
697	    return {
698	        "per_template": out_per_template,
699	        "win_count": win_count,                # Carney-only count (bar)
700	        "extra_win_count": extra_win_count,    # non-Carney wins (transparency)
701	        "loss_count": loss_count,
702	        "tie_count": tie_count,
703	        "insufficient_count": insufficient_count,
704	        "template_count": template_count,
705	        "carney_template_count": template_count - len(extra_templates),
706	        "missing_carney_templates": sorted(missing_carney),
707	        "extra_non_carney_templates": sorted(extra_templates),
708	        "verdict": verdict,
709	        "live_mode": LIVE_MODE,
710	        "comparable_dimensions": sorted(MATCH_OR_BEAT_DIMENSIONS),
711	        "required_carney_templates": sorted(CARNEY_TEMPLATES),
712	    }
713	
714	
715	def _nan_to_null(obj: Any) -> Any:
716	    """Replace NaN floats with None for standard JSON serialization.
717	    Per ECMA-404, JSON does not have NaN; bare NaN tokens emitted by Python's
718	    json.dumps default behavior are non-standard and break strict consumers."""
719	    if isinstance(obj, float):
720	        if obj != obj:   # NaN check
721	            return None
722	        return obj
723	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
724	        return {k: _nan_to_null(v) for k, v in obj.items()}
725	    if isinstance(obj, list):
726	        return [_nan_to_null(v) for v in obj]
727	    return obj
728	
729	
730	def main() -> int:
731	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS v6 internal benchmark runner")
732	    p.add_argument("--questions", type=Path, required=True, help="Path to question bank (JSON or YAML)")
733	    p.add_argument("--systems", nargs="+", default=list(SYSTEMS.keys()),
734	                   help=f"Systems to evaluate (subset of {list(SYSTEMS.keys())})")
735	    p.add_argument("--results-out", type=Path, default=RESULTS_PATH,
736	                   help=f"Output path for results JSON (default {RESULTS_PATH})")
737	    args = p.parse_args()
738	
739	    if not args.questions.is_file():
740	        print(f"questions file not found: {args.questions}", file=sys.stderr)
741	        return 1
742	
743	    questions = load_questions(args.questions)
744	    print(f"loaded {len(questions)} questions; running across systems: {args.systems}")
745	    print(f"LIVE_MODE={LIVE_MODE} (set POLARIS_BENCHMARK_LIVE=1 to enable real API calls)")
746	
747	    results = run_benchmark(questions, args.systems)
748	    aggregate = aggregate_per_system(results)
749	    per_template = aggregate_per_template(results)
750	    match_or_beat = compute_match_or_beat(per_template)
751	
752	    output = {
753	        "schema_version": "1.0.0",
754	        "run_timestamp": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
755	        "live_mode": LIVE_MODE,
756	        "rubric_path": str(RUBRIC_PATH.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()),
757	        "systems_evaluated": args.systems,
758	        "question_count": len(questions),
759	        "per_question_results": {
760	            qid: {
761	                "template": r.template,
762	                "responses": {s: asdict(resp) for s, resp in r.responses.items()},
763	                "scores": {s: [asdict(score) for score in scores] for s, scores in r.scores.items()},
764	            }
765	            for qid, r in results.items()
766	        },
767	        "aggregate_per_system": aggregate,
768	        "aggregate_per_template": per_template,
769	        "match_or_beat": match_or_beat,
770	    }
771	
772	    args.results_out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
773	    # Clean NaN floats → null for standard JSON (per ECMA-404)
774	    cleaned = _nan_to_null(output)
775	    args.results_out.write_text(json.dumps(cleaned, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
776	    try:
777	        rel = args.results_out.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()
778	    except ValueError:
779	        rel = str(args.results_out)
780	    print(f"results -> {rel}")
781	    print(f"aggregate per system: {json.dumps(aggregate, indent=2)}")
782	    return 0
783	
784	
785	if __name__ == "__main__":
786	    sys.exit(main())
787

1	"""Aggregate per-run cost from EvidenceContract bundles.
2	
3	Usage:
4	  python scripts/v6/cost_summary.py [--bundles-dir DIR] [--since YYYY-MM-DD]
5	
6	Walks every .json under --bundles-dir (default: outputs/runs/), parses each
7	as an EvidenceContract, and prints sum/avg/min/max/p50/p95 of cost_usd
8	across the matching set. With --since, only includes bundles whose
9	finished_at is >= the given ISO date.
10	
11	Used by the Carney handover runbook §5 (cost monitoring).
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import json
18	import statistics
19	import sys
20	from datetime import datetime
21	from pathlib import Path
22	from typing import Iterable
23	
24	from polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract import EvidenceContract
25	
26	
27	def _iter_bundle_paths(bundles_dir: Path) -> Iterable[Path]:
28	    if not bundles_dir.exists():
29	        return
30	    for path in sorted(bundles_dir.rglob("*.json")):
31	        if path.is_file():
32	            yield path
33	
34	
35	def _parse_since(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:
36	    if value is None:
37	        return None
38	    return datetime.fromisoformat(value)
39	
40	
41	def main() -> int:
42	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
43	    parser.add_argument(
44	        "--bundles-dir",
45	        type=Path,
46	        default=Path("outputs/runs"),
47	        help="Directory containing EvidenceContract JSON bundles (recursive).",
48	    )
49	    parser.add_argument(
50	        "--since",
51	        type=str,
52	        default=None,
53	        help="ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) — only include bundles finished_at >= this.",
54	    )
55	    args = parser.parse_args()
56	
57	    since = _parse_since(args.since)
58	
59	    costs: list[float] = []
60	    skipped = 0
61	    for path in _iter_bundle_paths(args.bundles_dir):
62	        try:
63	            contract = EvidenceContract.model_validate_json(
64	                path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
65	            )
66	        except Exception:
67	            skipped += 1
68	            continue
69	        if since is not None:
70	            try:
71	                finished = datetime.fromisoformat(
72	                    contract.finished_at.rstrip("Z")
73	                )
74	            except Exception:
75	                skipped += 1
76	                continue
77	            if finished < since:
78	                continue
79	        costs.append(float(contract.cost_usd))
80	
81	    if not costs:
82	        print(
83	            f"no bundles matched in {args.bundles_dir.resolve()}"
84	            + (f" since {args.since}" if args.since else "")
85	            + (f" ({skipped} skipped)" if skipped else "")
86	        )
87	        return 1
88	
89	    n = len(costs)
90	    print(f"bundles_dir: {args.bundles_dir.resolve()}")
91	    if since:
92	        print(f"since: {args.since}")
93	    print(f"matched_runs: {n}")
94	    print(f"sum_cost_usd: {sum(costs):.4f}")
95	    print(f"avg_cost_usd: {sum(costs) / n:.4f}")
96	    print(f"min_cost_usd: {min(costs):.4f}")
97	    print(f"max_cost_usd: {max(costs):.4f}")
98	    if n >= 2:
99	        print(f"p50_cost_usd: {statistics.median(costs):.4f}")
100	    if n >= 20:
101	        print(
102	            f"p95_cost_usd: {statistics.quantiles(costs, n=20)[-1]:.4f}"
103	        )
104	    if skipped:
105	        print(f"skipped_unparseable: {skipped}")
106	    return 0
107	
108	
109	if __name__ == "__main__":
110	    sys.exit(main())
111

1	"""Replay a previously saved pin and emit a PinDiff.
2	
3	Usage:
4	  python scripts/v6/replay_pin.py --original PATH --replay PATH [--json]
5	
6	Loads two RunPin JSON files and prints the field-level diff (or full JSON
7	with --json). Exits 1 if the diff flags any regression, 0 otherwise. Used
8	by the Carney handover runbook §6 (backup + replay).
9	
10	This script is the deterministic comparator — it does NOT regenerate the
11	replay pin. To regenerate a pin against new model weights, use
12	`scripts/v6/run_pin_replay.py`.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import argparse
18	import json
19	import sys
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	from polaris_v6.replay.differ import compute_pin_diff
23	from polaris_v6.replay.schema import RunPin
24	
25	
26	def main() -> int:
27	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
28	    parser.add_argument(
29	        "--original",
30	        type=Path,
31	        required=True,
32	        help="Path to the baseline RunPin JSON.",
33	    )
34	    parser.add_argument(
35	        "--replay",
36	        type=Path,
37	        required=True,
38	        help="Path to the replay RunPin JSON (post regen against new model).",
39	    )
40	    parser.add_argument(
41	        "--json",
42	        action="store_true",
43	        help="Emit the full PinDiff as JSON (default: human-readable summary).",
44	    )
45	    args = parser.parse_args()
46	
47	    original = RunPin.model_validate_json(args.original.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
48	    replay = RunPin.model_validate_json(args.replay.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
49	
50	    diff = compute_pin_diff(original, replay)
51	
52	    if args.json:
53	        print(diff.model_dump_json(indent=2))
54	    else:
55	        print(f"original_pin_id: {diff.original_pin_id}")
56	        print(f"replay_pin_id:   {diff.replay_pin_id}")
57	        print(f"is_regression:   {diff.is_regression}")
58	        print(
59	            f"verified_sentence_count_delta: "
60	            f"{diff.verified_sentence_count_delta:+d}"
61	        )
62	        print(f"pipeline_status_changed: {diff.pipeline_status_changed}")
63	        print(
64	            f"evidence_pool: +{len(diff.evidence_pool_added)} added, "
65	            f"-{len(diff.evidence_pool_dropped)} dropped"
66	        )
67	        print(f"fields_changed: {len(diff.fields_changed)}")
68	        for field in diff.fields_changed:
69	            print(
70	                f"  [{field.severity}] {field.field}: "
71	                f"{field.original!r} -> {field.replay!r}"
72	            )
73	
74	    return 1 if diff.is_regression else 0
75	
76	
77	if __name__ == "__main__":
78	    sys.exit(main())
79

1	"""POLARIS v6 benchmark runner — Phase 3 Task 3.5 substrate.
2	
3	Reads a benchmark question fixture file (BrowseComp / GAIA / DeepResearch
4	Bench JSON), maps each record through the appropriate adapter, then
5	either:
6	
7	- (Phase 0/1, NO cluster) Emits a stub `BenchmarkSuiteDesign` JSON skeleton
8	  so the user can inspect the schema before running anything for real.
9	- (Phase 3, cluster live) Each adapted question is sent to the running
10	  POLARIS instance, the response is graded against expected answer,
11	  results aggregated into a `BenchmarkSuiteDesign`.
12	
13	Phase 0 ships the dry-run mode only. Real execution requires a live
14	backend at `POLARIS_V6_BACKEND_URL`.
15	
16	Usage:
17	    python scripts/v6/run_benchmark.py --suite browsecomp --input bc.jsonl --dry-run
18	    python scripts/v6/run_benchmark.py --suite gaia --input gaia.jsonl --backend http://127.0.0.1:8000
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import json
25	import sys
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "src") not in sys.path:
29	    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "src"))
30	
31	from polaris_v6.benchmark.industry_adapters import IndustryBenchmark, adapt
32	from polaris_v6.benchmark.schema import (
33	    BenchmarkQuestion,
34	    BenchmarkSuiteDesign,
35	    DifficultyLevel,
36	)
37	
38	
39	def _load_records(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
40	    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
41	    if path.suffix == ".jsonl":
42	        return [json.loads(line) for line in text.splitlines() if line.strip()]
43	    return json.loads(text)
44	
45	
46	def _difficulty_from_metadata(metadata: dict[str, str]) -> DifficultyLevel:
47	    raw = (metadata.get("difficulty") or metadata.get("level") or "").lower()
48	    if raw in {"3", "hard", "novel_synthesis", "novel"}:
49	        return "novel_synthesis"
50	    if raw in {"adversarial", "trick"}:
51	        return "adversarial"
52	    return "routine"
53	
54	
55	def build_suite_from_records(
56	    *,
57	    suite: IndustryBenchmark,
58	    records: list[dict],
59	) -> BenchmarkSuiteDesign:
60	    questions: list[BenchmarkQuestion] = []
61	    for raw in records:
62	        adapted = adapt(suite, raw)
63	        questions.append(
64	            BenchmarkQuestion(
65	                question_id=f"{suite}:{adapted.question_id}",
66	                template="canada_us"
67	                if "canada" in adapted.question_text.lower()
68	                else "clinical",
69	                text=adapted.question_text,
70	                difficulty=_difficulty_from_metadata(adapted.metadata),
71	                expected_anchors=[adapted.expected_answer]
72	                if adapted.expected_answer
73	                else [],
74	            )
75	        )
76	    return BenchmarkSuiteDesign(
77	        suite_version=f"v6_phase3_{suite}_v1",
78	        questions=questions,
79	        competing_systems=["polaris_v6", "chatgpt_5_5_pro_dr", "gemini_3_1_pro_dr"],
80	        score_dimensions=[
81	            "factual_accuracy",
82	            "citation_health",
83	            "frame_coverage",
84	            "contradiction_handling",
85	            "refusal_calibration",
86	            "user_traceability",
87	        ],
88	        layer_3_evaluator_required=True,
89	    )
90	
91	
92	def main() -> int:
93	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
94	    parser.add_argument(
95	        "--suite",
96	        choices=["browsecomp", "gaia", "deepresearch_bench"],
97	        required=True,
98	    )
99	    parser.add_argument(
100	        "--input",
101	        type=Path,
102	        required=True,
103	        help="Benchmark question file (.json or .jsonl)",
104	    )
105	    parser.add_argument(
106	        "--output",
107	        type=Path,
108	        default=None,
109	        help="Where to write the BenchmarkSuiteDesign JSON. Defaults to stdout.",
110	    )
111	    parser.add_argument(
112	        "--dry-run",
113	        action="store_true",
114	        help="Build the suite design only; do not call the backend.",
115	    )
116	    parser.add_argument(
117	        "--backend",
118	        default=None,
119	        help="POLARIS backend URL. Required if not --dry-run; Phase 3 work.",
120	    )
121	    args = parser.parse_args()
122	
123	    if not args.input.exists():
124	        print(f"ERROR: input file not found: {args.input}", file=sys.stderr)
125	        return 2
126	
127	    records = _load_records(args.input)

1	"""Run regression lab — pair baseline pins with candidate pins and gate.
2	
3	Usage:
4	  python scripts/v6/run_pin_replay.py \
5	      --baseline-dir tests/v6/fixtures/baseline_pins/ \
6	      --candidate-dir outputs/replay/<date>/
7	
8	Loads every *.json under each directory as a RunPin, pairs by run_id,
9	runs the regression lab, prints a summary, and exits 1 if any regression
10	is detected. Used by the Carney handover runbook §3 (model rotation
11	gate) and by CI when new weights are swapped in.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import sys
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	from polaris_v6.regression_lab.runner import run_regression_lab
21	from polaris_v6.replay.schema import RunPin
22	
23	
24	def _load_pins(d: Path) -> list[RunPin]:
25	    pins: list[RunPin] = []
26	    for path in sorted(d.glob("*.json")):
27	        if not path.is_file():
28	            continue
29	        pins.append(RunPin.model_validate_json(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
30	    return pins
31	
32	
33	def main() -> int:
34	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
35	    parser.add_argument("--baseline-dir", type=Path, required=True)
36	    parser.add_argument("--candidate-dir", type=Path, required=True)
37	    args = parser.parse_args()
38	
39	    if not args.baseline_dir.exists():
40	        print(f"baseline-dir does not exist: {args.baseline_dir}")
41	        return 2
42	    if not args.candidate_dir.exists():
43	        print(f"candidate-dir does not exist: {args.candidate_dir}")
44	        return 2
45	
46	    baseline = _load_pins(args.baseline_dir)
47	    candidate = _load_pins(args.candidate_dir)
48	
49	    if not baseline:
50	        print(f"no baseline pins found in {args.baseline_dir}")
51	        return 2
52	
53	    report = run_regression_lab(baseline=baseline, candidate=candidate)
54	
55	    print(f"baseline pins: {report.baseline_count}")
56	    print(f"candidate pins: {report.candidate_count}")
57	    print(f"matched pairs: {report.matched_count}")
58	    print(f"regressions: {len(report.regressions)}")
59	    print(f"warns: {len(report.warns)}")
60	    if report.unmatched_baseline_pin_ids:
61	        print(
62	            f"unmatched_baseline_pin_ids ({len(report.unmatched_baseline_pin_ids)}): "
63	            f"{report.unmatched_baseline_pin_ids}"
64	        )
65	    if report.unmatched_candidate_pin_ids:
66	        print(
67	            f"unmatched_candidate_pin_ids ({len(report.unmatched_candidate_pin_ids)}): "
68	            f"{report.unmatched_candidate_pin_ids}"
69	        )
70	
71	    if report.regressions:
72	        print("\nREGRESSIONS:")
73	        for diff in report.regressions:
74	            print(
75	                f"  {diff.original_pin_id} -> {diff.replay_pin_id}: "
76	                f"{len(diff.fields_changed)} field(s) changed"
77	            )
78	
79	    print(f"\nverdict: {'PASS' if report.passed else 'FAIL'}")
80	    return 0 if report.passed else 1
81	
82	
83	if __name__ == "__main__":
84	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-rdy-018 (#514) — OpenRouter V4 Pro non-sovereign rehearsal runner.
3	
4	Phase-4 rehearsal: point the LLM endpoint at OpenRouter V4 Pro + Gemma
5	(test-only, non-confidential prompts) and exercise the full v6 journey.
6	
7	Subcommands:
8	  check-models   GET OpenRouter /models; confirm the generator (V4 Pro) and
9	                 evaluator (Gemma) model ids are available. No token spend.
10	  run            Run the fixed non-confidential prompt set
11	                 (tests/v6/fixtures/rehearsal_prompts.yaml) through the real
12	                 v6 actor path. `--dry-run` validates wiring with no spend.
13	
14	The live `run` path calls `enqueue_research_run.fn(...)` — the undecorated
15	v6 actor function — so the rehearsal exercises the genuine actor q-dict
16	(unique artifact dir + synthesized v30_contract_patch), not a shortcut.
17	
18	Usage:
19	    python scripts/v6/run_rehearsal.py check-models [--generator ID] [--evaluator ID]
20	    python scripts/v6/run_rehearsal.py run [--dry-run] [--only TEMPLATE]
21	        [--max-cost N] [--out-root DIR] [--generator ID] [--evaluator ID]
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import argparse
27	import json
28	import os
29	import sys
30	import urllib.error
31	import urllib.request
32	import uuid
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	# The actor module decorates @dramatiq.actor at import; force the StubBroker
36	# so importing it never needs a live Redis. The runner uses `.fn()`, never
37	# the broker, so this is inert beyond import-time.
38	os.environ.setdefault("POLARIS_V6_QUEUE_USE_STUB", "1")
39	
40	import yaml  # noqa: E402  (third-party; project dependency)
41	
42	DEFAULT_GENERATOR = "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"
43	DEFAULT_EVALUATOR = "z-ai/glm-5.1"  # I-arch-003 #1253: sovereign mirror (was stale gemma)
44	DEFAULT_MAX_COST = "5.00"
45	PROMPTS_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "tests" / "v6" / "fixtures" / "rehearsal_prompts.yaml"
46	
47	
48	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
49	    """Fail loud: stderr + non-zero exit (CLAUDE.md LAW II)."""
50	    raise SystemExit(f"[run_rehearsal] ERROR: {msg}")
51	
52	
53	def _openrouter_base() -> str:
54	    return os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1").rstrip("/")
55	
56	
57	def _load_prompts() -> list[dict]:
58	    if not PROMPTS_PATH.is_file():
59	        _fail(f"prompt set not found: {PROMPTS_PATH}")
60	    data = yaml.safe_load(PROMPTS_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
61	    prompts = data.get("prompts") if isinstance(data, dict) else None
62	    if not prompts:
63	        _fail(f"{PROMPTS_PATH.name} has no `prompts` list")
64	    for entry in prompts:
65	        if "template" not in entry or "question" not in entry:
66	            _fail(f"prompt entry missing template/question: {entry!r}")
67	    return prompts
68	
69	
70	def cmd_check_models(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
71	    key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
72	    if not key:
73	        _fail("OPENROUTER_API_KEY is unset — the rehearsal cannot run without it")
74	
75	    url = f"{_openrouter_base()}/models"
76	    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {key}"})
77	    try:
78	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
79	            catalogue = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
80	    except (urllib.error.URLError, json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:
81	        _fail(f"OpenRouter /models request failed: {exc}")
82	
83	    available = {m.get("id") for m in catalogue.get("data", []) if isinstance(m, dict)}
84	    wanted = {"generator": args.generator, "evaluator": args.evaluator}
85	    print(f"OpenRouter /models — {len(available)} models in catalogue")
86	    missing = []
87	    for role, model_id in wanted.items():
88	        present = model_id in available
89	        print(f"  [{'OK ' if present else 'MISSING'}] {role}: {model_id}")
90	        if not present:
91	            missing.append(model_id)
92	    if missing:
93	        _fail(f"configured model(s) not available on OpenRouter: {', '.join(missing)}")
94	    print("check-models: PASS — generator + evaluator both available")
95	
96	
97	def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
98	    from polaris_v6.queue.actors import TEMPLATE_TO_SCOPE_DOMAIN
99	
100	    prompts = _load_prompts()
101	    if args.only:
102	        prompts = [p for p in prompts if p["template"] == args.only]
103	        if not prompts:
104	            _fail(f"--only {args.only!r} matched no prompt in the set")
105	    for entry in prompts:
106	        if entry["template"] not in TEMPLATE_TO_SCOPE_DOMAIN:
107	            _fail(
108	                f"prompt template {entry['template']!r} is not one of the "
109	                f"canonical templates {sorted(TEMPLATE_TO_SCOPE_DOMAIN)}"
110	            )
111	
112	    key_present = bool(os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip())
113	    print("=" * 64)
114	    print("POLARIS v6 non-sovereign rehearsal" + ("  [DRY RUN]" if args.dry_run else ""))
115	    print("=" * 64)
116	    print(f"  generator        : {args.generator}")
117	    print(f"  evaluator        : {args.evaluator}")
118	    print(f"  backend          : openrouter ({_openrouter_base()})")
119	    print(f"  OPENROUTER_API_KEY: {'present' if key_present else 'ABSENT'}")
120	    print(f"  PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN: {args.max_cost}")
121	    print(f"  prompts          : {len(prompts)}")
122	    for entry in prompts:
123	        print(f"    - {entry['template']}: {entry['question']}")
124	    print("-" * 64)
125	
126	    if args.dry_run:
127	        print("DRY RUN: wiring validated — no LLM call, no run-store write, "
128	              "no spend. Re-run without --dry-run to execute the billed rehearsal.")
129	        return
130	
131	    if not key_present:
132	        _fail("OPENROUTER_API_KEY is unset — cannot execute a live rehearsal run")
133	
134	    # Wire the LLM endpoint as env vars (Phase-4: point at V4 Pro + Gemma).
135	    os.environ["PG_GENERATOR_MODEL"] = args.generator
136	    os.environ["PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL"] = args.evaluator
137	    os.environ["PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN"] = str(args.max_cost)
138	    if args.out_root:
139	        os.environ["POLARIS_V6_OUTPUT_ROOT"] = str(args.out_root)
140	
141	    from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
142	    from polaris_v6.queue.actors import enqueue_research_run
143	
144	    results: list[dict] = []
145	    for entry in prompts:
146	        template, question = entry["template"], entry["question"]
147	        run_id = f"rehearsal_{template}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
148	        run_store.insert_run(run_id, template, question)
149	        print(f"[run] {template} ({run_id}) ...")
150	        status = {"template": template, "run_id": run_id}
151	        try:
152	            enqueue_research_run.fn(run_id, {"template": template, "question": question})
153	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — record any pipeline crash, continue
154	            print(f"  EXCEPTION: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
155	        row = run_store.get_run(run_id, path=None)
156	        status["pipeline_status"] = getattr(row, "pipeline_status", None)
157	        status["cost_usd"] = getattr(row, "cost_usd", None)
158	        results.append(status)
159	        print(f"  pipeline_status={status['pipeline_status']} cost_usd={status['cost_usd']}")
160	
161	    # A run "passes start-to-finish" if the pipeline reached a terminal
162	    # verdict — success / abort_* / partial_* all count (CLAUDE.md §9.3:
163	    # abort_* are pipeline verdicts, not errors). Only error_* / a missing
164	    # status (crash before any verdict) fails the rehearsal.
165	    failed = [
166	        r for r in results
167	        if not r["pipeline_status"] or str(r["pipeline_status"]).startswith("error_")
168	    ]
169	    total_cost = sum(r["cost_usd"] or 0.0 for r in results)
170	    print("-" * 64)
171	    print(f"rehearsal: {len(results) - len(failed)}/{len(results)} prompts "
172	          f"reached a terminal verdict; total cost_usd={total_cost:.4f}")
173	    if failed:
174	        print(f"RESULT: FAIL — {len(failed)} prompt(s) did not pass: "
175	              f"{[r['template'] for r in failed]}")
176	        raise SystemExit(1)
177	    print("RESULT: PASS — the full non-sovereign rehearsal path passed start-to-finish")
178	
179	
180	def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
181	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS v6 OpenRouter rehearsal runner")
182	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
183	
184	    check = sub.add_parser("check-models", help="confirm V4 Pro + Gemma are available on OpenRouter")
185	    check.add_argument("--generator", default=DEFAULT_GENERATOR)
186	    check.add_argument("--evaluator", default=DEFAULT_EVALUATOR)
187	    check.set_defaults(func=cmd_check_models)
188	
189	    run = sub.add_parser("run", help="run the fixed non-confidential prompt set")
190	    run.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
191	                     help="validate wiring only — no LLM call, no spend")
192	    run.add_argument("--only", default=None, help="run a single template")
193	    run.add_argument("--max-cost", default=DEFAULT_MAX_COST,
194	                     help="PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN per run (default 5.00)")
195	    run.add_argument("--out-root", default=None, help="override POLARIS_V6_OUTPUT_ROOT")
196	    run.add_argument("--generator", default=DEFAULT_GENERATOR)
197	    run.add_argument("--evaluator", default=DEFAULT_EVALUATOR)
198	    run.set_defaults(func=cmd_run)
199	    return parser
200	
201	
202	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
203	    args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv)
204	    args.func(args)
205	
206	
207	if __name__ == "__main__":
208	    main()
209

1	"""I-carney-005 — POLARIS v6 preflight diagnostics.
2	
3	Runs inside the container; checks:
4	- Required env vars present
5	- Redis broker reachable
6	- GPG keyring contains the configured POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID
7	- run_store sqlite path writable
8	
9	Exits 0 on success, non-zero per failure type (LAW II: fail loud, not silent).
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import os
15	import sys
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	
19	REQUIRED_VARS_API = [
20	    "POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL",
21	    "POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB",
22	]
23	RECOMMENDED_VARS = [
24	    "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
25	    "SERPER_API_KEY",
26	    "POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID",
27	]
28	
29	
30	def check_env() -> list[str]:
31	    failures: list[str] = []
32	    for var in REQUIRED_VARS_API:
33	        if not os.environ.get(var):
34	            failures.append(f"missing required env var: {var}")
35	    for var in RECOMMENDED_VARS:
36	        if not os.environ.get(var):
37	            print(f"[preflight] WARN: recommended env var unset: {var}")
38	    return failures
39	
40	
41	def check_redis() -> list[str]:
42	    failures: list[str] = []
43	    url = os.environ.get("POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL", "redis://redis:6379/0")
44	    try:
45	        import redis  # noqa: PLC0415
46	        client = redis.from_url(url)
47	        client.ping()
48	        print(f"[preflight] redis reachable: {url}")
49	    except Exception as exc:
50	        failures.append(f"redis unreachable at {url}: {exc}")
51	    return failures
52	
53	
54	def check_gpg(strict: bool = True) -> list[str]:
55	    """Per Codex diff iter-1 P2: in a GPG-deploy preflight, POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID
56	    being unset is a HARD failure (signed bundles are the deploy's reason
57	    for existing). Set strict=False only for non-bundling subcommands.
58	    """
59	    failures: list[str] = []
60	    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip()
61	    if not key_id:
62	        msg = "POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID unset; signed bundles unavailable"
63	        if strict:
64	            failures.append(msg)
65	        else:
66	            print(f"[preflight] WARN: {msg}")
67	        return failures
68	    homedir = os.environ.get("GNUPGHOME", "/app/gpg")
69	    if not Path(homedir).exists():
70	        failures.append(f"GNUPGHOME {homedir} does not exist")
71	        return failures
72	    try:
73	        import gnupg  # noqa: PLC0415
74	        gpg = gnupg.GPG(gnupghome=homedir)
75	        keys = gpg.list_keys(secret=True, keys=[key_id])
76	        if not keys:
77	            failures.append(f"GPG key {key_id} not in keyring at {homedir}")
78	        else:
79	            print(f"[preflight] GPG key {key_id} present in {homedir}")
80	    except Exception as exc:
81	        failures.append(f"GPG check failed: {exc}")
82	    return failures
83	
84	
85	def check_run_db() -> list[str]:
86	    failures: list[str] = []
87	    db_path = os.environ.get("POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB", "/app/state/v6_runs.sqlite")
88	    parent = Path(db_path).parent
89	    try:
90	        parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
91	        # Touch a write probe to verify perms (idempotent).
92	        probe = parent / ".preflight_probe"
93	        probe.write_text("ok")
94	        probe.unlink()
95	        print(f"[preflight] run_db parent writable: {parent}")
96	    except Exception as exc:
97	        failures.append(f"run_db parent {parent} not writable: {exc}")
98	    return failures
99	
100	
101	def main() -> int:
102	    all_failures: list[str] = []
103	    all_failures.extend(check_env())
104	    all_failures.extend(check_redis())
105	    all_failures.extend(check_gpg())
106	    all_failures.extend(check_run_db())
107	
108	    if all_failures:
109	        print("\n[preflight] FAILED:")
110	        for f in all_failures:
111	            print(f"  - {f}")
112	        return 1
113	    print("\n[preflight] all checks passed")
114	    return 0
115	
116	
117	if __name__ == "__main__":
118	    sys.exit(main())
119

1	"""End-to-end live verification of slice 004 audit-bundle signing.
2	
3	Builds a minimal valid {decision, pool, report} payload, POSTs to
4	/api/audit-bundle, writes the returned tarball, extracts the .asc

10	signature. No middle ground.

112	def _verify_tarball(tar_bytes: bytes, verbose: bool) -> int:
113	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
114	        td_path = Path(td)
115	        tar_path = td_path / "bundle.tar.gz"
116	        tar_path.write_bytes(tar_bytes)
117	
118	        if verbose:
119	            print(f"  bundle bytes: {len(tar_bytes)}")
120	            print(f"  written to: {tar_path}")
121	
122	        with tarfile.open(tar_path, "r:gz") as tf:
123	            members = tf.getnames()
124	            tf.extractall(td_path / "extracted")
125	        if verbose:
126	            print(f"  members: {members}")
127	
128	        ext = td_path / "extracted"
129	        # Find the signed manifest + signature
130	        sig_files = list(ext.rglob("*.asc"))
131	        if not sig_files:
132	            print("  FAIL: no .asc signature in tarball", file=sys.stderr)
133	            return 2
134	        sig_path = sig_files[0]
135	        manifest_path = sig_path.with_suffix("")  # strip .asc
136	        if not manifest_path.is_file():
137	            print(
138	                f"  FAIL: signed manifest missing: {manifest_path}",
139	                file=sys.stderr,
140	            )
141	            return 3
142	        if verbose:
143	            print(f"  signature: {sig_path.relative_to(ext)} "
144	                  f"({sig_path.stat().st_size} bytes)")
145	            print(f"  manifest:  {manifest_path.relative_to(ext)} "
146	                  f"({manifest_path.stat().st_size} bytes)")
147	
148	        # gpg --verify <sig> <manifest>
149	        proc = subprocess.run(
150	            ["gpg", "--verify", str(sig_path), str(manifest_path)],
151	            capture_output=True, text=True,
152	        )
153	        if verbose:
154	            print(f"  gpg rc: {proc.returncode}")
155	            print(f"  gpg stderr (first 3 lines):")
156	            for line in proc.stderr.splitlines()[:3]:
157	                print(f"    {line}")
158	        if proc.returncode != 0:
159	            print(
160	                f"  FAIL: gpg --verify returned {proc.returncode}",
161	                file=sys.stderr,
162	            )
163	            return 4
164	        if "Good signature" not in proc.stderr:
165	            print(
166	                f"  FAIL: gpg verified but did not say 'Good signature':\n"
167	                f"  {proc.stderr}",
168	                file=sys.stderr,
169	            )
170	            return 5
171	    return 0
172	
173	
174	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
175	    verbose = "-v" in argv or "--verbose" in argv
176	    load_dotenv()
177	    print("Slice 004 audit-bundle live verification")
178	    print("==========================================")
179	
180	    # Lazy imports so missing keys surface as 503 not import error
181	    from polaris_v6.api.app import create_app
182	
183	    app = create_app()
184	    client = TestClient(app)
185	
186	    # Health check
187	    h = client.get("/api/audit-bundle/health").json()
188	    print(f"  signing_backend: {h.get('signing_backend')!r}")
189	    if h.get("signing_backend") != "gpg":
190	        print(
191	            "  FAIL: expected signing_backend='gpg' (set POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID "
192	            "in .env and run scripts/setup_gpg_for_demo.py first)",
193	            file=sys.stderr,
194	        )
195	        return 1
196	
197	    payload = _build_payload()
198	    if verbose:
199	        print(f"  payload size: {len(json.dumps(payload))} bytes")
200	
201	    print("[1/2] POST /api/audit-bundle")
202	    r = client.post("/api/audit-bundle", json=payload)
203	    if r.status_code != 200:
204	        print(
205	            f"  FAIL: status={r.status_code} body={r.text[:300]}",
206	            file=sys.stderr,
207	        )
208	        return 1
209	    tar_bytes = r.content
210	    print(f"  OK   {len(tar_bytes)} bytes returned")
211	
212	    print("[2/2] gpg --verify on signed manifest")
213	    rc = _verify_tarball(tar_bytes, verbose)
214	    if rc != 0:
215	        return rc
216	
217	    print("==========================================")
218	    print("AUDIT BUNDLE E2E PASSED: real GPG signature verifies")
219	    return 0
220	
221	
222	if __name__ == "__main__":
223	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""POLARIS cage verification — runs at session start and on demand.
3	
4	Verifies the cage established 2026-05-04 per polaris-controls/PLAN.md is
5	intact:
6	
7	  1. POLARIS branch protection on `polaris` and `main` matches expected
8	     baseline (signed commits, no force push, no deletions).
9	  2. polaris-controls branch protection on `main` matches expected
10	     baseline (signed commits, code-owner review, 1 approval).
11	  3. session_pin.txt SHAs match live blob SHAs of pinned files in
12	     polaris-controls HEAD.
13	  4. CODEOWNERS file present and covers expected control-plane paths.
14	
15	Output: structured pass/fail with per-check detail. Exits 0 on full pass,
16	1 on any failure.
17	
18	Usage:
19	    python scripts/verify_cage.py
20	    python scripts/verify_cage.py --json             # machine-readable
21	    python scripts/verify_cage.py --polaris-only     # skip cross-repo
22	
23	Dependencies: gh CLI authenticated as aldrinor with metadata read on both
24	repos. No third-party Python packages required.
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import argparse
30	import json
31	import re
32	import subprocess
33	import sys
34	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any
37	
38	REPO_OWNER = "aldrinor"
39	POLARIS_REPO = "polaris"
40	CONTROLS_REPO = "polaris-controls"
41	POLARIS_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
42	
43	EXPECTED_PROTECTION_POLARIS = {
44	    "required_signatures.enabled": True,
45	    "allow_force_pushes.enabled": False,
46	    "allow_deletions.enabled": False,
47	}
48

50	    "required_signatures.enabled": True,
51	    "enforce_admins.enabled": True,
52	    "allow_force_pushes.enabled": False,
53	    "allow_deletions.enabled": False,

78	@dataclass
79	class CheckResult:
80	    name: str
81	    passed: bool
82	    detail: str = ""
83	
84	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
85	        return asdict(self)

89	class Report:
90	    passed: bool = True
91	    checks: list[CheckResult] = field(default_factory=list)

94	        self.checks.append(CheckResult(name, passed, detail))
95	        if not passed:
96	            self.passed = False

100	            "passed": self.passed,

105	def gh(args: list[str]) -> tuple[int, str, str]:
106	    result = subprocess.run(
107	        ["gh"] + args, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False
108	    )

111	
112	def get_protection(owner: str, repo: str, branch: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
113	    rc, out, _ = gh(["api", f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/{branch}/protection"])
114	    if rc != 0:
115	        return None
116	    try:

118	    except json.JSONDecodeError:

122	def get_blob_sha(owner: str, repo: str, path: str) -> str | None:
123	    rc, out, _ = gh(
124	        ["api", f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}", "--jq", ".sha"]
125	    )
126	    if rc != 0:
127	        return None

130	
131	def nested_get(obj: Any, dotted: str) -> Any:
132	    cur = obj
133	    for part in dotted.split("."):
134	        if isinstance(cur, dict) and part in cur:
135	            cur = cur[part]
136	        else:
137	            return None
138	    return cur
139	
140	
141	def check_protection(
142	    report: Report,
143	    owner: str,
144	    repo: str,
145	    branch: str,
146	    expected: dict[str, Any],
147	) -> None:
148	    label = f"{repo}/{branch}"
149	    bp = get_protection(owner, repo, branch)
150	    if bp is None:
151	        report.add(
152	            f"protection.{label}.exists",
153	            False,
154	            f"no branch protection found (or unauthorized to fetch)",
155	        )
156	        return
157	    report.add(f"protection.{label}.exists", True)
158	    for key, want in expected.items():
159	        actual = nested_get(bp, key)
160	        ok = actual == want
161	        report.add(
162	            f"protection.{label}.{key}",
163	            ok,
164	            f"expected {want!r}, got {actual!r}" if not ok else "",
165	        )

168	def check_session_pin(report: Report) -> None:
169	    pin_path = POLARIS_ROOT / "docs" / "session_pin.txt"

171	        report.add(
172	            "session_pin.exists", False,
173	            f"file not found at {pin_path}",
174	        )
175	        return
176	    report.add("session_pin.exists", True)
177	
178	    pin_text = pin_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
179	    pinned: dict[str, str] = {}
180	    for line in pin_text.splitlines():
181	        line = line.strip()
182	        if not line or line.startswith("#"):
183	            continue
184	        m = re.match(r"^([\w_]+):\s*([\w-]+)\s*$", line)
185	        if m:
186	            pinned[m.group(1)] = m.group(2)
187	
188	    for key, controls_path in PIN_KEY_TO_PATH.items():
189	        recorded = pinned.get(key)
190	        if not recorded:
191	            report.add(
192	                f"session_pin.{key}.recorded", False,
193	                f"key '{key}' missing from session_pin.txt",
194	            )
195	            continue
196	        if recorded.startswith("TBD"):
197	            report.add(
198	                f"session_pin.{key}.recorded", False,
199	                f"placeholder TBD value: {recorded!r}",
200	            )
201	            continue
202	        report.add(f"session_pin.{key}.recorded", True)
203	
204	        live = get_blob_sha(REPO_OWNER, CONTROLS_REPO, controls_path)
205	        if live is None:
206	            report.add(
207	                f"session_pin.{key}.live_fetch", False,
208	                f"could not fetch live blob SHA for {controls_path}",
209	            )
210	            continue
211	        ok = recorded == live
212	        report.add(
213	            f"session_pin.{key}.matches",
214	            ok,
215	            f"recorded {recorded[:12]}..., live {live[:12]}..." if not ok else "",
216	        )
217	
218	
219	def check_codeowners(report: Report) -> None:
220	    co_path = POLARIS_ROOT / ".github" / "CODEOWNERS"
221	    if not co_path.is_file():
222	        report.add("codeowners.exists", False, f"not found at {co_path}")

224	    report.add("codeowners.exists", True)
225	
226	    raw = co_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
227	    for path in EXPECTED_CODEOWNERS_PATHS:
228	        present = path in raw
229	        report.add(
230	            f"codeowners.covers[{path}]",
231	            present,
232	            f"path '{path}' not found in CODEOWNERS" if not present else "",
233	        )
234	
235	
236	def emit(report: Report, as_json: bool) -> None:
237	    if as_json:
238	        print(json.dumps(report.to_dict(), indent=2))

240	
241	    print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")

248	        if c.detail:

251	    overall = "PASSED" if report.passed else "FAILED"
252	    print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
253	    print(f"OVERALL: {overall}")

256

1	"""
2	verify_route_coverage.py — route-coverage gate for codex-visual-required.
3	
4	Given a list of changed files (one path per line on stdin) and a
5	manifest produced by visual_review_gate.py, verifies that:
6	
7	  * Every `web/app/<segments>/page.tsx` change in the PR maps to a
8	    route present in the manifest's `route:` field. Next.js convention
9	    is followed: `web/app/foo/bar/page.tsx` → `/foo/bar`; dynamic
10	    segments like `[runId]` match any concrete value in the manifest.
11	
12	  * `web/components/**` changes are reported as advisory — they can be
13	    used in any page, so per-file route coverage is undecidable without
14	    a build-time import graph. The operator is expected to list the
15	    affected pages explicitly in the brief; the gate logs them.
16	
17	  * `web/app/<...>/layout.tsx`, `web/app/<...>/template.tsx`,
18	    `web/app/<...>/route.ts` etc. that are NOT `page.tsx` are also
19	    advisory — they affect any page below their directory, so coverage
20	    is operator-declared not auto-derivable.
21	
22	Exits 0 on full coverage, 1 on any uncovered `page.tsx` route, 2 on
23	input error.
24	
25	Codex iter-3 P1 fix: closes the gap where a current-head audit could
26	cover an unrelated route and still satisfy the manifest-walk check.
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import argparse
32	import json
33	import re
34	import sys
35	from pathlib import Path
36	
37	
38	PAGE_RE = re.compile(r"^web/app/(?P<segments>.*?)/?page\.tsx$")
39	DYNAMIC_SEG_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^/\]]+\]")
40	
41	
42	def changed_page_route(path: str) -> str | None:
43	    """Map web/app/<segments>/page.tsx → /<segments>.
44	
45	    `web/app/page.tsx` → `/`
46	    `web/app/inspector/[runId]/page.tsx` → `/inspector/[runId]`
47	    `web/app/(group)/dashboard/page.tsx` → `/dashboard` (Next.js group)
48	    """
49	    m = PAGE_RE.match(path)
50	    if not m:
51	        return None
52	    segments = m.group("segments")
53	    if not segments:
54	        return "/"
55	    # Drop Next.js (group) segments — they don't affect URL.
56	    parts = [p for p in segments.split("/") if not (p.startswith("(") and p.endswith(")"))]
57	    if not parts:
58	        return "/"
59	    return "/" + "/".join(parts)
60	
61	
62	def matches_manifest_route(declared_route: str, manifest_routes: list[str]) -> bool:
63	    """`declared_route` may contain `[runId]`-style dynamic segments.
64	
65	    A manifest route matches if, segment-by-segment:
66	      - declared is `[<name>]`  → any concrete segment in manifest matches
67	      - declared is concrete    → must equal the manifest segment exactly
68	    """
69	    declared_parts = [p for p in declared_route.split("/") if p]
70	    for mroute in manifest_routes:
71	        m_parts = [p for p in mroute.split("/") if p]
72	        if len(m_parts) != len(declared_parts):
73	            continue
74	        ok = True
75	        for d, m in zip(declared_parts, m_parts):
76	            if DYNAMIC_SEG_RE.fullmatch(d):
77	                # dynamic — accept any non-empty concrete
78	                if not m:
79	                    ok = False
80	                    break
81	            elif d != m:
82	                ok = False
83	                break
84	        if ok:
85	            return True
86	    return False
87	
88	
89	def discover_all_app_routes(repo_root: Path) -> list[str]:
90	    """Enumerate every web/app/<segments>/page.tsx in the repo.
91	
92	    Returns the derived route list (Next.js convention). Used to require
93	    a conservative full-route sweep when the PR touches non-page UI
94	    surface (web/components/** or layout/template/loading/error/
95	    not-found.tsx) — those changes affect undetermined pages, so the
96	    only mechanically-enforceable contract is "audit every page".
97	    """
98	    routes: set[str] = set()
99	    app_dir = repo_root / "web" / "app"
100	    if not app_dir.exists():
101	        return []
102	    for page in app_dir.rglob("page.tsx"):
103	        rel = page.relative_to(repo_root).as_posix()
104	        r = changed_page_route(rel)
105	        if r is not None:
106	            routes.add(r)
107	    return sorted(routes)
108	
109	
110	def main() -> int:
111	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__.splitlines()[0])
112	    p.add_argument("--manifest", required=True, help="path to manifest.json")
113	    p.add_argument(
114	        "--changed-files",
115	        required=False,
116	        help="path to file with changed paths (one per line); defaults to stdin",
117	    )
118	    p.add_argument(
119	        "--repo-root",
120	        default=".",
121	        help="repo root for discovering all page.tsx routes (default: cwd)",
122	    )
123	    args = p.parse_args()
124	
125	    mpath = Path(args.manifest)
126	    if not mpath.exists():
127	        print(f"ERROR: manifest not at {mpath}", file=sys.stderr)
128	        return 2
129	    try:
130	        entries = json.loads(mpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
131	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
132	        print(f"ERROR: manifest not valid JSON: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
133	        return 2
134	    if not isinstance(entries, list):
135	        print("ERROR: manifest is not a list", file=sys.stderr)
136	        return 2
137	
138	    manifest_routes = sorted({e.get("route") for e in entries if e.get("route")})
139	    if not manifest_routes:
140	        print("ERROR: manifest has no route fields", file=sys.stderr)
141	        return 2
142	
143	    if args.changed_files:
144	        changed = Path(args.changed_files).read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
145	    else:
146	        changed = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
147	    changed = [c.strip() for c in changed if c.strip()]
148	
149	    uncovered: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
150	    broad_changes: list[str] = []
151	    page_changes: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
152	
153	    for path in changed:
154	        if path.startswith("web/app/") and path.endswith("page.tsx"):
155	            route = changed_page_route(path)
156	            if route is None:
157	                continue
158	            page_changes.append((path, route))
159	            if not matches_manifest_route(route, manifest_routes):
160	                uncovered.append((path, route))
161	        elif path.startswith("web/components/"):
162	            broad_changes.append(path)
163	        elif path.startswith("web/app/") and (
164	            path.endswith("layout.tsx")
165	            or path.endswith("template.tsx")
166	            or path.endswith("loading.tsx")
167	            or path.endswith("error.tsx")
168	            or path.endswith("not-found.tsx")
169	        ):
170	            broad_changes.append(path)
171	
172	    print(f"Manifest routes: {len(manifest_routes)}")
173	    for r in manifest_routes:
174	        print(f"  {r}")
175	    print(f"Page changes: {len(page_changes)}")
176	    for path, route in page_changes:
177	        covered = "OK" if (path, route) not in uncovered else "MISS"
178	        print(f"  [{covered}] {path} -> {route}")
179	
180	    # Codex iter-4 P1 fix: broad changes (components, layouts,
181	    # templates, loading/error/not-found) require a CONSERVATIVE
182	    # full-route sweep. The manifest must cover every page.tsx route
183	    # discovered in the repo. This closes the bypass where a
184	    # `web/components/proof_replay/proof_replay.tsx` change could be
185	    # audited against an unrelated route like `/intake`.
186	    full_sweep_missing: list[str] = []
187	    if broad_changes:
188	        print(f"Broad UI changes (component / layout / template / loading / error / not-found): {len(broad_changes)}")
189	        for c in broad_changes:
190	            print(f"  {c}")
191	        repo_root = Path(args.repo_root).resolve()
192	        all_routes = discover_all_app_routes(repo_root)
193	        print(f"All page.tsx routes discovered in repo: {len(all_routes)}")
194	        for r in all_routes:
195	            covered_in_manifest = matches_manifest_route(r, manifest_routes)
196	            marker = "OK" if covered_in_manifest else "MISS"
197	            print(f"  [{marker}] {r}")
198	            if not covered_in_manifest:
199	                full_sweep_missing.append(r)
200	
201	    if uncovered:
202	        print("ERROR: route coverage missing — these page changes are not in the manifest:")
203	        for path, route in uncovered:
204	            print(f"  {path} -> {route}")
205	        return 1
206	
207	    if full_sweep_missing:
208	        print(
209	            "ERROR: broad UI changes (components / layouts / etc) require a"
210	            " FULL-ROUTE SWEEP. These page.tsx routes exist in the repo but"
211	            " are NOT in the manifest:"
212	        )
213	        for r in full_sweep_missing:
214	            print(f"  {r}")
215	        print(
216	            "Re-run scripts/visual_review_gate.py with --routes covering"
217	            " every discovered route, OR scope the PR to a single page so"
218	            " per-page coverage applies."
219	        )
220	        return 1
221	
222	    print("OK: every changed page.tsx route is covered; broad changes (if any) have full-route sweep")
223	    return 0
224	
225	
226	if __name__ == "__main__":
227	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""
2	verify_screenshot_manifest.py — helper called from codex-visual-required.yml.
3	
4	Reads the manifest produced by visual_review_gate.py at
5	`outputs/visual_review_gate/<id>/iter_N/manifest.json` and verifies:
6	- the manifest has >= 1 entry (P1-iter2 fix)
7	- every `<label>.png` exists at the same directory
8	- every file's sha256 matches the manifest entry's `sha256` field
9	
10	Exits 0 on success, 1 on any drift. Logs each failure on its own line.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import hashlib
16	import json
17	import pathlib
18	import sys
19	
20	
21	def main() -> int:
22	    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
23	        print("usage: verify_screenshot_manifest.py <manifest.json> <screenshots_dir>", file=sys.stderr)
24	        return 2
25	
26	    manifest_path = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1])
27	    screenshots_dir = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[2])
28	
29	    if not manifest_path.is_file():
30	        print(f"ERROR: manifest not at {manifest_path}", file=sys.stderr)
31	        return 1
32	
33	    try:
34	        entries = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
35	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
36	        print(f"ERROR: manifest is not valid JSON: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
37	        return 1
38	
39	    if not isinstance(entries, list) or len(entries) < 1:
40	        print(f"ERROR: manifest at {manifest_path} is empty or not a list", file=sys.stderr)
41	        return 1
42	
43	    errors: list[str] = []
44	    for entry in entries:
45	        label = entry.get("label")
46	        declared = entry.get("sha256")
47	        if not label or not declared:
48	            errors.append(f"manifest entry malformed (missing label/sha256): {entry}")
49	            continue
50	        p = screenshots_dir / f"{label}.png"
51	        if not p.exists():
52	            errors.append(f"screenshot file missing: {p}")
53	            continue
54	        h = hashlib.sha256()
55	        with p.open("rb") as f:
56	            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(65536), b""):
57	                h.update(chunk)
58	        actual = h.hexdigest()
59	        if actual != declared:
60	            errors.append(
61	                f"screenshot SHA drift: {p} declared={declared} actual={actual}"
62	            )
63	
64	    if errors:
65	        print("ERROR: manifest verification failed:")
66	        for e in errors:
67	            print(f"  {e}")
68	        return 1
69	
70	    print(f"OK: all {len(entries)} screenshots present with matching SHA256")
71	    return 0
72	
73	
74	if __name__ == "__main__":
75	    sys.exit(main())
76
